questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

The PLI scheme is aimed at:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: project / the / completed / was / time / on
options: The project was completed on time
Was the project completed on time
Completed was the project on time
On time the project was completed
questions.solution: The project was completed on time

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Coal is the most abundant fossil fuel in India and plays a crucial role in the country's energy mix. India has the fourth-largest coal reserves in the world, with an estimated 344 billion tonnes. Coal India Limited (CIL) is the largest coal-producing company globally, accounting for about 82% of India's coal output. However, concerns about environmental degradation and climate change have prompted a shift towards cleaner energy sources like solar and wind power. The government has set a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

According to the passage, India has the ____ largest coal reserves in the world.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: plays / role / coal / in / energy / a / vital / production / India
options: Coal plays a vital role in India energy production
Vital role coal plays in energy production India
In India coal plays a energy vital production role
Coal in India plays vital role energy production
questions.solution: Coal plays a vital role in India energy production

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Underground mining is one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. The primary risks include roof falls, gas explosions, flooding, and ventilation failures. In India, the Mines Act of 1952 and the Coal Mines Regulations of 2017 govern safety standards. Modern technology has significantly improved mine safety through real-time monitoring systems, gas detectors, and automated equipment. Despite these advances, safety remains a critical concern, and regular training programs are mandatory for all mine workers. The introduction of continuous miners and longwall mining methods has increased both productivity and safety.

Continuous miners and longwall mining methods have increased:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: innovation / for / key / is / growth / economic
options: Innovation is key for economic growth
Economic growth is key for innovation
For economic growth innovation is key
Key for growth economic is innovation
questions.solution: Innovation is key for economic growth

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Underground mining is one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. The primary risks include roof falls, gas explosions, flooding, and ventilation failures. In India, the Mines Act of 1952 and the Coal Mines Regulations of 2017 govern safety standards. Modern technology has significantly improved mine safety through real-time monitoring systems, gas detectors, and automated equipment. Despite these advances, safety remains a critical concern, and regular training programs are mandatory for all mine workers. The introduction of continuous miners and longwall mining methods has increased both productivity and safety.

Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a risk in underground mining?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: climate / urgent / change / requires / action / addressing
options: Addressing climate change requires urgent action
Climate change addressing urgent action requires
Requires urgent action change addressing climate
Urgent action climate change requires addressing
questions.solution: Addressing climate change requires urgent action

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Coal is the most abundant fossil fuel in India and plays a crucial role in the country's energy mix. India has the fourth-largest coal reserves in the world, with an estimated 344 billion tonnes. Coal India Limited (CIL) is the largest coal-producing company globally, accounting for about 82% of India's coal output. However, concerns about environmental degradation and climate change have prompted a shift towards cleaner energy sources like solar and wind power. The government has set a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

The word 'abundant' in the passage most nearly means:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: data / protect / essential / is / privacy / to
options: Essential is to protect data privacy
Data privacy is essential to protect

questions.stem:
Passage: Underground mining is one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. The primary risks include roof falls, gas explosions, flooding, and ventilation failures. In India, the Mines Act of 1952 and the Coal Mines Regulations of 2017 govern safety standards. Modern technology has significantly improved mine safety through real-time monitoring systems, gas detectors, and automated equipment. Despite these advances, safety remains a critical concern, and regular training programs are mandatory for all mine workers. The introduction of continuous miners and longwall mining methods has increased both productivity and safety.

The word 'mandatory' in the passage most nearly means:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: environmental / concerns / need / planning / careful

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Coal is the most abundant fossil fuel in India and plays a crucial role in the country's energy mix. India has the fourth-largest coal reserves in the world, with an estimated 344 billion tonnes. Coal India Limited (CIL) is the largest coal-producing company globally, accounting for about 82% of India's coal output. However, concerns about environmental degradation and climate change have prompted a shift towards cleaner energy sources like solar and wind power. The government has set a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

What percentage of India's coal output does CIL account for?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: education / the / to / key / is / success
options: Education is the key to success
To success the key is education
Success is the key to education
questions.solution: Education is the key to success

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

India's solar power capacity in 2024 is approximately:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: company / its / announced / expansion / has / plans / the
options: The company has announced its expansion plans
Has the company announced its expansion plans
Expansion plans the company has announced
Announced the company its expansion plans has
questions.solution: The company has announced its expansion plans

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Gondwana coal fields of eastern India contain the country's best quality coal. These fields stretch across Jharkhand, West Bengal, Odisha, and Chhattisgarh. The coal found here is primarily bituminous, which has high calorific value and low ash content. In contrast, the Tertiary coal fields of northeastern India contain lignite and sub-bituminous coal. Coal grading is based on ash content, with higher grades having lower ash percentages. Washeries are used to reduce ash content and improve coal quality before it is supplied to power plants and industries.

Tertiary coal fields of northeastern India contain:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of PERILOUS:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: Neither he nor his brother are going to the party.
options: Neither he nor his brother were going to the party.
Neither he nor his brother are going to the party.
Neither he nor his brother have been going to the party.
Neither he nor his brother is going to the party.
questions.solution: Neither he nor his brother is going to the party.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The price of (A) / these goods (B) / have increased (C) / significantly. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is one of those who has excelled in the field.
options: He is one of those who have excelled in the field.
He is one who has excelled in the field.
He is one of those who excelled in the field.
He is one of those who has excelled in the field.
questions.solution: He is one of those who have excelled in the field.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The company (A) / is in (B) / the red (C) / for three years. (D)
options: D - should be 'has been in the red for three years'
questions.solution: D - should be 'has been in the red for three years'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She is the tallest of the two sisters.
options: She is the taller of the two sisters.
She is the tallest of the two sisters.
She is more tall of the two sisters.
She is tall among the two sisters.
questions.solution: She is the taller of the two sisters.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The car (A) / which he (B) / is driving (C) / is a Mercedes. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of FERVENT:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Intrinsic semiconductor carrier concentration ni for Si at 300K:
questions.solution: ni(Si, 300K) ≈ 1.5×10¹⁰ cm⁻³. Depends on temperature exponentially.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 8085 interrupt vectored interrupts in priority order:
questions.solution: TRAP (highest, NMI), RST7.5, RST6.5, RST5.5, INTR (lowest, needs external hardware).

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Active power filter purpose:
options: Compensates for harmonics and reactive power drawn by nonlinear loads
questions.solution: APF: injects compensating currents. Real-time harmonic elimination. Active vs passive filtering.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: FIR linear phase condition: impulse response must be:
questions.solution: Linear phase: h[n] = h[N-1-n] (symmetric) or h[n] = -h[N-1-n] (antisymmetric). Ensures constant group delay.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Wave impedance of free space:
questions.solution: η₀ = √(μ₀/ε₀) = 120π ≈ 377 Ω. Ratio of E to H in free-space plane wave.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Watchdog timer: generates reset when:
questions.solution: WDT: counts down. If not refreshed (kicked) before timeout, generates system reset. Safety mechanism.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Data acquisition system: anti-aliasing filter placed before:
questions.solution: AA filter: analog low-pass before ADC. Removes frequencies above Nyquist (fs/2) to prevent aliasing. Critical for accurate digitization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inverting amp: Rf=22kΩ, Rin=22kΩ, Vin=3V. Vout:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coherent detection of BPSK requires:
questions.solution: Coherent BPSK: multiply received signal by local carrier (same phase). Costas loop for recovery.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A multiplexer (MUX) is a circuit that:
options: Selects one of several inputs onto a single output
Splits one input into many outputs
questions.solution: A MUX routes one of 2ⁿ inputs to the output based on n select lines.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Doping Si with phosphorus creates:
questions.solution: P has 5 valence electrons. 4 bond with Si, 1 free electron → N-type (negative carriers).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shift register parallel load uses:
questions.solution: PIPO: data loaded in parallel, output read in parallel. Used in registers.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Thermal voltage Vt at 300K:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Decoder:
options: n inputs → 2^n outputs. Active high or low. 3:8 decoder
questions.solution: Decoder: 74LS138 (3:8). Used for memory address decoding, demultiplexing.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Diffusion current is due to:
questions.solution: Diffusion: carriers move from high to low concentration. Einstein relation: D/μ = kT/q.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DAC types:
options: Weighted resistor (binary weights), R-2R ladder (equal resistors, more accurate)
questions.solution: R-2R: uses only two resistor values. Resolution: n bits. Settling time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Intrinsic semiconductor:
options: Pure semiconductor with equal electron and hole concentrations (n = p = n_i)
questions.solution: Si: n_i ≈ 1.5×10¹⁰ cm⁻³ at 300K. GaAs: n_i ≈ 1.8×10⁶ cm⁻³.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Linear time-invariant system transfer function H(z) with all zeros inside unit circle is:
questions.solution: Min phase: all zeros inside |z|=1. Max phase: all zeros outside. Mixed: both.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AM carrier of amplitude 80 V is modulated by a 30 V message. The modulation index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Si band gap at 300K:
questions.solution: Eg: Si=1.12, Ge=0.66, GaAs=1.42, GaN=3.4 eV at 300K.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A signal has a maximum frequency of 4 kHz. Its Nyquist sampling rate is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A link transmits 40 W with a net antenna gain of 100 and a path loss factor of 1000. The received power is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An n-type semiconductor is produced by doping silicon with a:
questions.solution: Pentavalent atoms (e.g. phosphorus) donate free electrons, giving n-type material.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Anti-aliasing filter:
options: Low-pass filter before sampling (removes frequencies above Nyquist)
questions.solution: Anti-aliasing: prevents frequencies > f_s/2 from folding. Usually active filter (e.g., Butterworth).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Channel models — Rayleigh:
options: No line-of-sight (NLOS). Scattering. Fading amplitude: Rayleigh distribution
questions.solution: Rayleigh: multipath, no dominant path. Ricean: LOS present. K-factor = LOS power/scatter power.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A p-type semiconductor is produced by doping silicon with a:
questions.solution: Trivalent atoms (e.g. boron) create holes, giving p-type material.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The sampling theorem (Nyquist-Shannon):
options: A bandlimited signal can be reconstructed from samples at f_s ≥ 2f_max
questions.solution: Reconstruction: ideal low-pass filter. Aliasing when f_s < 2f_max (folded frequencies).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Antenna gain:
questions.solution: Gain in dBi: G_dBi = 10log₁₀(G_linear). Half-wave dipole: G ≈ 2.15 dBi. Isotropic: 0 dBi.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: BJT: Vcc=10V, Rb=470kΩ, β=50. Collector current Ic:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Phase margin:
options: 180° + ∠G(jω)H(jω) at gain crossover (|GH| = 1). PM > 0 for stable
questions.solution: PM: how much phase lag can be added before instability. Typically 45-60°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Non-inverting: Rf=47kΩ, R₁=2.2kΩ, Vin=0.5V. Vout:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Characteristic equation: s²+2ζω_n s+ω_n²=0. Damping ratio ζ=0.707 gives:
questions.solution: ζ=0.707: %OS = exp(-ζπ/√(1-ζ²))×100 ≈ 4.3%. Underdamped but near critically damped.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Doubling the number of bits in an ADC changes the number of quantization levels from 2ⁿ to:
questions.solution: Levels = 2^bits, so doubling n gives 2^(2n) = (2ⁿ)² levels.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: NAND and NOR are termed universal gates because they can:
questions.solution: Any logic function can be implemented using only NAND (or only NOR) gates.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An ADC is a circuit that converts:
options: An analog signal into a digital code
questions.solution: An analog-to-digital converter samples and quantizes an analog input into digital values.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FM modulation index β = Δf/f_m. For Δf=75kHz, f_m=15kHz:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An inverting op-amp has Rf = 22 kΩ and Rin = 2 kΩ. Its voltage gain is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Nyquist criterion: N = Z - P, where N is number of encirclements of (-1,j0). For stable system:
options: N = -P (all poles of G(s) in LHP)
questions.solution: Z = N + P. For Z=0 (stable CL): N = -P. Count clockwise encirclements.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shift register types:
options: SISO, SIPO, PISO, PIPO. Serial/parallel input/output
questions.solution: Shift: left, right, bidirectional. 74LS164 (SIPO), 74LS165 (PISO). Ring counter: Q fed to D_in.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PAL (Programmable Array Logic) has:
options: Programmable AND array, fixed OR array
questions.solution: PAL: programmable AND (product terms) → fixed OR. PLA: both programmable. PROM: fixed AND, programmable OR.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Demultiplexer (DEMUX):
options: Routes single input to one of 2^n outputs
questions.solution: DEMUX: decoder with enable. 1:4, 1:8. Used for data distribution.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DRAM stores each bit as:
questions.solution: DRAM: 1T1C cell. Capacitor stores charge. Must refresh periodically.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ring counter:
options: One '1' circulating. n stages = n states. Decoded output (no decoder needed)
questions.solution: Ring: n states from n FFs. Johnson (twisted ring): 2n states. Switch-tail.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FPGA architecture:
options: Look-up tables (LUTs), flip-flops, DSP blocks, block RAM, I/O, clock management
questions.solution: FPGA: configurable logic. HDL design (Verilog/VHDL). Synthesis → map → place → route. Bitstream.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Setup and hold time:
options: Setup: data must be stable before clock. Hold: data stable after clock. Violation → metastability
questions.solution: Setup time: FF input stabilization. Hold: transparency prevents data bypass. Critical in sync design.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Moore vs Mealy state machines:
options: Moore: output depends only on state. Mealy: output depends on state + input
questions.solution: Mealy: fewer states (output on transition). Moore: one more clock delay. Glitches in Mealy.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Op-amp — CMRR:
options: Common Mode Rejection Ratio. CMRR = 20log(A_d/A_cm) dB. Rejects common-mode signals
questions.solution: CMRR: high = good. 80-120 dB for precision op-amps. Instrumentation amplifier: very high CMRR.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The slew rate of an op-amp limits the:
options: Maximum rate of change of the output voltage
questions.solution: Slew rate (V/µs) bounds how fast the output can swing, distorting fast large signals.

options: Infinite gain, infinite input impedance, zero output impedance, infinite bandwidth
questions.solution: Ideal: V_+ = V_- (virtual short), I_in = 0. Practical: A_OL ~ 10^5-10^7.

options: LC tank with tapped inductor (inductive divider feedback)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge

options: Converts active input line to binary code (n inputs → log₂n outputs)
questions.solution: Encoder: 8:3, 10:4 (decimal to BCD). Priority encoder: handles simultaneous inputs.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge

questions.solution: 8085: no indexed or relative addressing. 8086 adds indexed, based, based-indexed.
